key_insight: A single “cliff token” can abruptly trigger mathematical reasoning failure in LLMs, and identifying that token enables recovery and targeted improvement.
engineering_relevance: For LLM reasoning systems, pinpointing failure-trigger tokens offers a practical control point for debugging, reranking, and single-token preference optimization to improve answer reliability.
method: Detect cliff tokens using a token-wise potential drop with an adaptive threshold based on local potential and a one-sided two-proportion z-test.
Classify cliffs into deterministic, uncertain, and sampled-off types using greedy choice and token entropy.
Apply single-token preference optimization at cliff positions (Cliff-DPO) to test whether correcting those tokens improves reasoning.
evidence_signal: Across seven models and three benchmarks (GSM1K, MATH500, AIME 2025), deleting the first cliff token and resampling recovered pass@64 to 1.0, and Cliff-DPO trained on GSM8K improved benchmark accuracy by up to +6.6.
watch_item: The cliff concept and its gains are validated on specific benchmarks and training settings, so broader robustness and cost across diverse tasks still need confirmation.
paper_title: Cliff Tokens: Identifying Single-Token Failure Triggers in LLM Mathematical Reasoning
